channel.title: NYT > New York
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Mamdani Allies Sweep House Primaries in Big Night for Left-Wing Democrats
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani backed Darializa Avila Chevalier, who beat Representative Adriano Espaillat, and Brad Lander, who ousted Representative Dan Goldman.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times

item.title: Customers Sue Madison Square Garden Over Hacking of 26 Million Records
item.description: Three class-action lawsuits accuse a hacking group of publishing data that included personal and corporate information stolen from the arena.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: Madison Square Garden received a ransom demand this month from ShinyHunters, a hacking group, that said it had stolen data.

item.title: A Last-Minute Guide to the Candidates in New York’s Primaries
item.description: There are more than half a dozen competitive races underway. Here’s what to know.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos, Davaughnia Wilson and Ashley Southall
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: After nine days of early voting, Primary Day arrived in New York City.

item.title: ‘This Is the Team,’ Mamdani Says. His Allies Hint Lander Doesn’t Belong.
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed three people for Congress in New York City, but some closest to him have chosen to push only Claire Valdez and Darializa Avila Chevalier.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani appeared with Brad Lander, the former New York City comptroller running against Representative Dan Goldman, on Tuesday.

item.title: A Primary in Upstate New York Tests G.O.P. Voters’ Loyalty to Trump
item.description: State party leaders are backing Assemblyman Robert Smullen. But a local businessman and provocateur, Anthony Constantino, has President Trump’s endorsement.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Adrianna Newell for The New York Times
item.media:description: New York State Republican leaders are overwhelmingly backing Robert Smullen, a former Marine, to replace Representative Elise Stefanik, who is not seeking re-election.

item.title: Goldman Says Cafe’s Hostile Post About Him Does Not Warrant Civil Rights Case
item.description: After a Justice Department official said her office was opening an investigation, Representative Dan Goldman said the department should focus on “other people in the Jewish community who are really scared.”
item.dc:creator: Miles G. Cohen and Olivia Bensimon
item.media:credit: Laura Brett/Getty Images
item.media:description: Representative Dan Goldman said that a Brooklyn cafe’s hostile social media post about him did not warrant a federal civil rights investigation.

item.title: A Glimmer of Hope for Fixing Penn Station
item.description: The plans that Amtrak has released for redoing the notorious station offers good architecture and some promise of moving ahead, our architecture critic says.
item.dc:creator: James Barron
item.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times

item.title: $50 Million Spent on N.Y. Primaries Includes A.I. and AIPAC Money
item.description: As early voting began earlier this month, AIPAC emerged from the sidelines to funnel $650,000 to Representative Adriano Espaillat, a candidate opposed by a democratic socialist.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Adriano Espaillat’s long support of Israel and his acceptance of AIPAC money have become attack lines for his primary opponent, Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: Manhattan Borough President Gives $50 Million to N.Y.C. Arts Groups
item.description: In allocating the borough’s discretionary budget entirely to cultural projects, Brad Hoylman-Sigal said he wanted to send a message to President Trump about the need to keep arts funding.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small
item.category: LaGuardia, Fiorello H, High School of Music and Performing Arts
Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture

item.title: Pregnant and Running for Re-election, a Democrat Faces U.S. Prosecution
item.description: Representative LaMonica McIver was charged with assault after an altercation with immigration agents that resulted in no injuries. She faces up to 17 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative LaMonica McIver.

item.title: Dianna Russini Was an N.F.L. ‘Insider.’ Was She Also Out of Bounds?
item.description: Ms. Russini’s closeness to a coach, Mike Vrabel of the New England Patriots, led to her breakup with The Athletic and its parent, The New York Times Company.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman and Ken Belson

item.title: Exonerated New Yorkers Say Compensation Process Is Traumatic
item.description: They can receive millions of dollars from cities and the state. But sometimes, they must first go on trial again.
item.dc:creator: Willow Higgins and Hurubie Meko
item.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Irons spent a majority of his life in prison after falsely confessing to a murder. He found himself back in a courtroom to win a lawsuit against the state of New York for his wrongful conviction.

item.title: Three Parties, Two Boroughs, One Message: Mamdani’s Gamble Pays Off
item.description: From Brooklyn to Manhattan, Mayor Zohran Mamdani made stops at primary victory parties for the three congressional candidates on his slate.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani made appearances at three victory parties on Tuesday, the last with Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: Insurgency Over Incumbency: 7 Takeaways From New York’s Primaries
item.description: All three congressional candidates who were backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani won in Tuesday’s primary, bolstering his political movement.
item.dc:creator: Maya King, Sally Goldenberg and Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the victory of state Assemblywoman Claire Valdez, right, in a Democratic primary for a congressional seat.

item.title: Mamdani Emerges as Kingmaker, Pushing His Slate to a Primary Sweep
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani shook the Democratic establishment by helping drive three progressive candidates to victory.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: Brad Lander, center right, was one of the three congressional candidates backed by Mayor Zohran Mandani who won their primaries on Tuesday.

item.title: Schlossberg’s Defeat Dampens Dream of a Renewed Camelot
item.description: Jack Schlossberg fell well short of his bid to win a highly contested Democratic primary in Manhattan, his campaign hurt by staff defections and missed meetings.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley and Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jack Schlossberg, with his father, Edwin Schlossberg, right, saw his early polling lead evaporate.

item.title: Who Is Darializa Avila Chevalier?
item.description: Ms. Avila Chevalier pulled off one of the biggest upsets in a New York House primary since Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez defeated Joseph Crowley in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani gave strong support to Darializa Avila Chevalier in her challenge to the Democratic incumbent.

item.title: Darializa Avila Chevalier, a Mamdani Ally, Ousts Espaillat in Primary
item.description: The 32-year-old activist built a robust ground game powered by support from Mayor Zohran Mamdani. She would double the number of democratic socialists in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Maya King
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: Darializa Avila Chevalier, a 32-year-old activist, unseated a veteran congressman with Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s support.

item.title: Trump-Backed Sticker Magnate Wins House Primary in New York
item.description: Anthony Constantino defeated Assemblyman Robert Smullen, the state Republican Party’s chosen candidate, in a right-leaning upstate district.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Adrianna Newell for The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthony Constantino had the endorsement of President Trump.

item.title: Hard-Liner Wins G.O.P. Nomination for Governor in Maryland
item.description: The Republican primary victory of Dan Cox sets up a rematch with Wes Moore, who defeated Mr. Cox handily four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson
item.media:credit: Steve Ruark/Associated Press
item.media:description: Dan Cox, the Republican gubernatorial candidate in Maryland, at a campaign event in 2022 when he previously ran for the office.

item.title: Fired Navy Admiral Wins Democratic Runoff in South Carolina’s 1st District
item.description: Democrats expect Nancy Lacore to run a competitive general election despite the district’s Republican leaning.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: Micah Lasher Wins Manhattan Primary, Defeating Kennedy Heir and Others
item.description: Mr. Lasher, the chosen successor of Representative Jerrold Nadler, beat a field of Democratic candidates that included a fellow state assemblyman in a race for a coveted open seat.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Micah Lasher won the Democratic primary for a House seat in District 12 in Manhattan on Tuesday.

item.title: Cait Conley Wins Primary to Face Mike Lawler in N.Y. Swing District
item.description: Flipping the Hudson Valley seat in November would be an important victory for Democrats as they seek to take back control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cait Conley won the Democratic nomination for a New York House seat in the Hudson Valley.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Claire Valdez
item.description: Ms. Valdez, a relatively new state lawmaker in New York, won her House primary after a boost from Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who had recruited her to run.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Claire Valdez was one of three candidates endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who has sought to push elevate leftist candidates to Congress.

item.title: Claire Valdez, a Socialist Backed by Mamdani, Wins Key New York Primary
item.description: Ms. Valdez, a first-term state lawmaker, defeated the Brooklyn borough president, Antonio Reynoso, to win an open House seat in a district divided along demographic fault lines.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Claire Valdez, a state assemblywoman, won an early endorsement from Mayor Zohran Mamdani that helped her overcome some of the institutional support that went to Antonio Reynoso.

item.title: Thomas DiNapoli Beats 2 Challengers in N.Y. Comptroller Primary
item.description: Mr. DiNapoli, who was challenged from the left by Raj Goyle and Drew Warshaw, is on track to become the longest serving comptroller in New York history.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: Thomas DiNapoli has served as New York State comptroller since 2007.

item.title: Brad Lander Wins N.Y. House Primary in Which Israel Became a Top Issue
item.description: Mr. Lander, an ally of Mayor Zohran Mamdani, beat Representative Dan Goldman, a two-term incumbent Democrat, in a Lower Manhattan and Brooklyn district.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: Brad Lander won the Democratic primary for a House seat in District 10 in New York.

item.title: Polling in New York’s 12th District Points to a Competitive Race
item.description: Micah Lasher and Alex Bores have consistently led in recent polls as Jack Schlossberg’s support has dipped.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: Micah Lasher, left, and Alex Bores at a forum in Manhattan in March.

item.title: In New York’s Seventh Congressional District, Demographics Are Fueling a Competitive Race
item.description: A Democratic primary featuring Antonio Reynoso, Claire Valdez and Julie Won could be defined by the recent influx of young white voters to the district.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler and Emma Schartz
item.media:credit: Adam Gray and Ryan Murphy for The New York Times

item.title: Here Are 8 New York State Legislative Races to Watch
item.description: The left and moderate wings of the Democratic Party are fighting it out in several races around New York City.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters, volunteers and members of the New York City Democratic Socialists of America at a joint campaign rally earlier this month for the candidates they are backing in Assembly races.

item.title: Democratic socialists took New York’s City Hall. Now they’re aiming at Congress.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Madison Swart, Victor J. Blue, & Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Claire Valdez, Conrad Blackburn and Darializa Avila Chevalier, from left to right, are being backed by the Democratic Socialists of America in New York City.

item.title: If Democrats want to win the House, their battle might start in the Hudson Valley.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

item.title: Ethnicity Becomes Instrument of Division in Espaillat’s Re-election Bid
item.description: Darializa Avila Chevalier has been targeted with false accusations about her ethnicity, as foes seek to portray her as an outsider hoping to usurp Dominican power in the district.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Darializa Avila Chevalier, the daughter of Dominican immigrants, has faced false accusations attempting to exploit the historical enmity between the Dominican Republic and Haiti.

item.title: A Kennedy scion has found it difficult to overcome some of his perceived shortcomings.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg and Claire Fahy
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jack Schlossberg, a grandson of John F. Kennedy, polled behind other candidates his House primary.

item.title: A New York House campaign became a proxy fight over artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

item.title: Brad Lander Isn’t an Incumbent. But He Has Run Like One.
item.description: Mr. Lander has longstanding ties to the district in parts of Brooklyn and Lower Manhattan where he is challenging Representative Dan Goldman.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: Eric Lee and Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times

item.title: A Challenger to Ritchie Torres Struggled to Gain Traction
item.description: Mr. Torres, who represents a House district in the Bronx, vastly out-raised Michael Blake, a former mayoral candidate.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Ritchie Torres is a former City Council member who represents one of the poorest congressional districts in America.

item.title: Why Neither Candidate for Governor of New York Faces a Primary Challenge
item.description: Six months ago, Gov. Kathy Hochul expected to have to run in a primary, and it looked like two leading state Republicans would be fighting for their party’s nod. Then things changed.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie/The New York Times
item.media:description: Elise Stefanik announced a run for governor but changed her mind when she didn’t get President Trump’s endorsement.

item.title: Social media posts have overshadowed a New York House primary.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg and Maya King
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: Darializa Avila Chevalier is running in the Democratic primary in New York’s 13th Congressional District in Upper Manhattan and parts of the Bronx.

item.title: Lin-Manuel Miranda’s ‘Warriors’ Musical to Hit Broadway Next Spring
item.description: Miranda is co-writing the musical — his first since “Hamilton” — with Eisa Davis. It’s based on “The Warriors” film and novel.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Daniel Weiss for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lin-Manuel Miranda and Eisa Davis have jointly written the book, music and lyrics for their new musical, “Warriors.”

item.title: Polling Is Limited in New York’s Democratic House Primaries
item.description: Few independent surveys were published in Democratic primaries despite prominent candidates and some ideological tests.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: New Yorkers cast ballots at P.S. 56 in Clinton Hill, Brooklyn, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Administration Sues New York State Over ICE Mask Ban
item.description: A Department of Justice lawsuit said that a measure prohibiting law enforcement officers from covering their faces would endanger agents.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní
item.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times

item.title: Micah Lasher is betting that a ‘nerd’ can prevail in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Micah Lasher, right, is one of several candidates running to fill a rare open seat left by Representative Jerrold Nadler’s retirement in a Manhattan district.

item.title: 2026 Jimmy Awards: With Bowen Yang as Host, Teen Winners Are Crowned
item.description: Students from Georgia and Arizona won the top prizes at the ceremony, which was hosted by Bowen Yang and celebrates excellence in high school musical theater.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: ‘The Sound of Music’ and ‘A Few Good Men’ Are Coming to Broadway
item.description: Lincoln Center Theater, basking in the glow of its Tony-winning “Ragtime” run, plans revivals of two more well-known titles this season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Natasha Moustache/Getty Images

item.title: Israel came to dominate a New York Democratic primary campaign.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.media:credit: Eduardo Munoz/Reuters
item.media:description: At the Israel Day parade in Manhattan, Representative Dan Goldman was in attendance, while his primary challenger, Brad Lander, was not.

item.title: The N.Y. Primaries Will Answer Key Questions: Mamdani, Israel and More
item.description: The congressional primaries in New York on Tuesday will answer key questions about the potency of super PACs and the extent of Zohran Mamdani’s influence.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Zohran Mamdani won the New York City mayoralty last year with more votes than any candidate in decades.

item.title: A tense New York primary reignited concerns over who gets to define the left.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Antonio Reynoso, the Brooklyn borough president, has been backed by Representative Nydia Velázquez, whom he is running to replace when she retires.

item.title: The strength of Mamdani’s political brand will be tested on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos and Sally Goldenberg
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City has aggressively pushed to help elevate like-minded candidates to Congress.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos and Emma G. Fitzsimmons

item.title: Plays, Musicals and Theater Festivals Across the U.S. Worth Traveling For
item.description: Across the country, audiences will find an abundance of Shakespeare, exciting new plays, and musicals and regional repertories in bucolic settings.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes and Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: New York Stage and Film Co
Muny (Municipal Theatre Association of St Louis)
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: The opening moment of the play “As You Like It” at Hudson Valley Shakespeare in Garrison, N.Y.
